nativeRange.habitat: Humid riparian and streamside microhabitats of tropical and subtropical East and Southeast Asia, growing as an epiphyte on rocks, tree trunks, and submerged or emergent wood

decorPreferences: branching driftwood (showcases the cascading triangular fronds)
vertical rock faces for cascading display
stainless or plastic mesh for moss walls and carpets
tied with thread/fishing line or cyanoacrylate gel until rhizoids anchor
gentle steady flow to discourage debris and algae between fronds

summary: An East Asian Taxiphyllum cultivar prized in aquascaping for its distinctive cascading, triangular fan-shaped fronds, which set it apart from the irregular bushy growth of its sister species Java Moss. Easy, low-light, CO2-optional epiphyte best displayed on branching wood or vertical rock.
careNotes: Taxiphyllum alternans, sold in the trade as Taiwan Moss (and as Dennerle's cultivar 'Taiwan'), is one of several aquarium mosses in the genus Taxiphyllum (sister to Java Moss T. barbieri (fw-plant-033), Christmas Moss Vesicularia montagnei, Flame Moss, and Spiky Moss). Its trademark feature is GROWTH FORM: fronds branch in a regular alternating pattern and form distinctive triangular fans that cascade downward, in clear visual contrast to the irregular, bushy mat of Java Moss. ATTACHMENT: Taiwan Moss is an epiphyte — tie or glue it to driftwood, porous rock, lava rock, or plastic/stainless mesh; do NOT bury it in substrate. Rhizoids anchor the moss to the surface over several weeks, after which thread or fishing line can be removed. Buce Plant's care notes (via Aquarium Co-Op's Mini Taiwan tissue-culture product) emphasize that 'attaching to branching wood or vertical rock will present it at its best,' allowing the horizontal, overhanging cascade to develop fully. LIGHTING is tolerant of low to medium levels; the moss survives in low-light setups, and moderate light produces denser growth with more pronounced triangular branching. Direct sunlight should be avoided as it encourages algae. CO2 is optional — the plant thrives without it, but moderate injection (10–40 mg/L per Dennerle and AquariumLesson) yields denser, more compact, and more brightly colored growth. GROWTH RATE is slow across all sources, consistent with most aquarium mosses; trimming controls shape. TEMPERATURE tolerance is reported across a very wide band: AquariumLesson cites 12–34 °C as the extreme survivable range; Dennerle recommends 22–28 °C for cultivation; hobby consensus surfaced via WebSearch sits at roughly 15–28 °C (59–82 °F) with a tropical preference; we record 20–28 °C as the typical aquarium-keeping range. pH 5.0–8.0 is the broad tolerated range (5.5–7.5 recorded as the typical aquarium target). HARDNESS: soft to hard water accepted (0–30 °dGH, 0–14 °dKH). FERTILIZATION demand is low — the moss absorbs nutrients from the water column and does not require a nutrient-rich substrate; Dennerle's reference dosing (NO3 10–50, PO4 0.1–3, K 5–30, Fe 0.01–0.5 mg/L) is generous and reflects high-tech setup optima rather than minimum requirements. SHRIMP AND FRY REFUGE: dense Taiwan Moss mats provide excellent microhabitat for newly hatched fry and for dwarf-shrimp larvae, which graze biofilm and microfauna from the fronds. PARAMETER SPREAD ACROSS SOURCES: temperature 12–34 °C (AquariumLesson) vs 22–28 °C (Dennerle) vs 15–28 °C (hobby consensus) — averaged to 20–28 °C for the schema; pH 5–8 confirmed by both AquariumLesson and Dennerle, narrowed to 5.5–7.5 for typical aquarium use. SOURCING NOTE: the originally-assigned Buce Plant product page (https://buceplant.com/products/taiwan-moss) returned HTTP 404 at fetch time, and Aquarium Breeder's care guide also returned 404. Aquarium Co-Op (Mini Taiwan tissue-culture variant) and Wikipedia's Taxiphyllum genus article were used together with AquariumLesson, Dennerle Plants, and a WebSearch synthesis (Bantam, Fish Laboratory, HYGRONATURE, Fluvico, Pet Zone, APF) to meet cross-verification per the playbook's primary-failure fallback rule.
breedingNotes: Vegetative propagation only in aquarium conditions: cut or pull off any healthy portion of the mat (or divide daughter plants per Dennerle) and re-attach it to a new surface — every fragment regrows. There is no need to wait for any reproductive cycle. True sexual reproduction (spore production from a sporophyte) requires emersed growth and is essentially never observed in submerged aquarium tanks.

primary.name: Aquarium Co-Op — Taxiphyllum alternans Taiwan (Tissue Culture)
primary.notes: Promoted to primary after the originally-assigned Buce Plant product page (https://buceplant.com/products/taiwan-moss) returned HTTP 404. Used for: scientific name confirmation (Taxiphyllum alternans), trade names (Taiwan Moss, Mini Taiwan Moss), low-demand easy care level ('easy and decorative moss with quite low demands'), low-to-good light tolerance, CO2 optional but promotes growth and branching ('some added CO2 will promote growth and branching'), epiphyte placement ('attaching to branching wood or vertical rock will present it at its best'), use for moss walls, and growth-form description ('growth of Mini Taiwan moss is horizontal and overhanging, like a mini version of Weeping moss').

additional.name: Wikipedia — Taxiphyllum (genus)
additional.notes: Taxonomic authority. Used for: family Hypnaceae, order Hypnales, genus description ('medium to large sized and mat forming. Stems are glossy and creeping'), confirmation that T. alternans is one of approximately 45 Taxiphyllum species, and confirmation of the genus's broad distribution across Asia, the Americas, Africa, and Pacific/Indian Ocean islands. Wikipedia provided no T. alternans-specific morphology, so distinguishing features (cascading triangular fans vs Java Moss bushy form) were drawn from the assignment notes and corroborated by Dennerle and the WebSearch hobby synthesis.

additional.name: AquariumLesson — Taxiphyllum alternans Taiwan Moss
additional.notes: Quantitative care reference. Used for: temperature 12–34 °C (extreme survivable band), pH 5–8, KH 0–14 °dKH, low light requirement, CO2 recommended at 10–40 mg/L, slow growth, foreground/midground placement, very easy care level, propagation by cutting daughter plants. Averaged with Dennerle's recommended ranges and the broader hobby consensus to produce the 20–28 °C / pH 5.5–7.5 schema values.

additional.name: Dennerle Plants — Taxiphyllum alternans 'Taiwan'
additional.notes: Commercial cultivator profile. Used for: native range (South East Asia), recommended temperature 22–28 °C, pH 5–8, low-to-medium light, CO2 10–20 mg/L, slow growth rate, foreground placement, max height ~5 cm, soft-to-hard water tolerance, propagation by division, family Hypnaceae, reference fertilization dosing (NO3 10–50, PO4 0.1–3, K 5–30, Fe 0.01–0.5 mg/L) and 'creeping moss with dense branching and soft structure' growth-form description.

additional.name: WebSearch synthesis — multiple Taiwan Moss hobby care guides
additional.notes: Additional cross-verification via search synthesis across Bantam.earth, Fish Laboratory, HYGRONATURE, Fluvico, Pet Zone Tropical Fish (San Diego), and Aquarium Plants Factory. Consistent consensus: temperature 15–28 °C (59–82 °F), pH 5.0–8.0 with 6.5–7.5 preferred, GH 0–30 °dGH, low-to-moderate light (direct sunlight discouraged), CO2 not required but moderate injection boosts growth, beginner-friendly, steady gentle flow recommended to prevent algae and aid propagation. Used to confirm that the AquariumLesson + Dennerle ranges are typical of the broader hobby consensus.

additional.name: Unavailable sources (documented for traceability)
additional.notes: Originally-assigned primary source Buce Plant (https://buceplant.com/products/taiwan-moss) returned HTTP 404 Not Found; sourcing was reassigned to Aquarium Co-Op per the playbook's primary-failure fallback rule. Additionally, Aquarium Breeder (https://aquariumbreeder.com/taiwan-moss-care-guide/) was attempted as a quantitative care reference (it was used successfully for the Java Moss sibling entry fw-plant-033) but also returned HTTP 404. 2H Aquarist's Taxiphyllum alternans care sheet (https://2hraquarist.com/blogs/plant-care-sheets/taxiphyllum-alternans) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden. All three are documented here so future contributors know they were tried and failed.

propagation: drape over branching driftwood or vertical rock to showcase cascading triangular fans
